title: podocarpus macrophyllus subsp. maki
intro: Podocarpus macrophyllus subsp. maki, also known as Maki or Japanese plum pine, is a subspecies of the Podocarpus macrophyllus plant, which is a type of coniferous evergreen tree native to East Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Maki plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, among other benefits. In herbal preparations, Maki leaves and seeds are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions that are traditionally used in traditional medicine to promote health and well-being.
